docs.title: MacSoccle
docs.text: A laser-cut bamboo base plate ("socle") for a MacBook Pro 16" M1, with integrated storage for 4 Samsung T7 SSDs and a Satechi USB-C hub.

docs.title: What is it?
docs.text: A flat plate that sits under a MacBook Pro 16", matching its exact footprint (355.7 x 248.1 mm) and corner rounding. It hides 4 SSDs and a USB-C hub inside, connected with short cables, keeping the desk clean. Only one USB-C cable exits the soccle to connect to the MacBook.

docs.title: Construction
docs.text:
4 layers of laser-cut bamboo plywood, total height ~13 mm:

Layer Thickness Function 1 (bottom) 1.5 mm Solid bottom plate 2 5 mm Internal frame with cutouts 3 5 mm Internal frame with cutouts 4 (top) 1.5 mm Removable top plate (velcro mount)

Layers 1-3 are glued. Layer 4 is removable for SSD access. Inside faces of layers 1 and 4 are lined with aluminium foil for heat spreading.

docs.title: Internal components
docs.text:
• 4x Samsung T7 SSD — 85 x 57 x 8 mm each
  • 1x Satechi 4-Port USB-C Hub (ST-H4CPDM) — 60 x 60 x 10 mm

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• Soccle design — full design spec, dimensions, component layout
  • Component layout — exact coordinates and cable routing
  • Laser cutting specs — Snijlab layer names, colors, file formats
  • Design workflow — how to create a design for laser cutting
  • Material — bamboo plywood properties and pricing

docs.title: Manufacturing
docs.text: Laser cutting by Snijlab (Netherlands). Files exported as DXF at 1:1 scale in mm.

docs.title: Creating a design for laser cutting
Workflow
docs.text:
1. Develop your concept (sketch, photo, idea)
  2. Create a vector drawing in a supported program
  3. Apply correct line colors and layer names (see specs)
  4. Export as DXF, DWG, AI or PDF
  5. Upload to Snijlab

docs.title: Supported software
docs.text:
• Adobe Illustrator
  • AutoCAD
  • Rhinoceros
  • Inkscape
  • Solidworks
  • Onshape
  • SketchUp
  • Vectorworks

docs.title: Design rules
docs.text:
• Design at full size (1:1 scale), units in mm
  • Maximum sheet size: 1200 x 600 mm
  • Convert all text to vector outlines
  • Remove unnecessary lines (dimensions, frames, centerlines)
  • Use correct layer names and line colors (see specs)

docs.title: Box generators
docs.text:
• MakerCase — use 0.2 mm burn offset
  • Boxes.py — use 0.2 mm burn offset

docs.title: Templates
docs.text:
Snijlab provides pre-made drawing templates that can be modified for common shapes and joints.

cf How do I create a design for laser cutting?

docs.title: DXF file format for Snijlab
docs.text: Learnings from analyzing Snijlab-template-crate.dxf.

docs.title: DXF version
docs.text:
• Version: AC1021 (AutoCAD 2007)
  • Use ezdxf.new("R2007") or ezdxf.new("R2010") in Python

docs.title: Units
docs.text: Header variable Value Meaning $INSUNITS 4 millimeters $MEASUREMENT 1 metric $LUNITS 2 decimal $LUPREC 3 3 decimal places

docs.title: Layers
docs.text:
Layer name Color Purpose vector-cut 5 (blue) All cut lines — this is what the laser cuts notes 18 (gray) Annotations, dimensions, visual guides (not cut) 0 7 (white) Default layer (unused in template)

Key insight: Snijlab uses vector-cut as the layer name (not just cut). Color is set on the layer, not on individual entities. All entities use color=BYLAYER.

docs.title: Entity types
docs.text:
The template uses only these entity types on the vector-cut layer:

  • POLYLINE (2D) — all 11 cut shapes are closed polylines
  • No LINE, CIRCLE, ARC, or SPLINE on the cut layer

docs.title: Polyline conventions
docs.text:
• All cut polylines are closed (is_closed=True)
  • Rounded corners use bulge values on vertices:
    • bulge = 0.4142 → 90° arc (quarter circle), used for standard rounded corners
    • bulge = 2.4142 → 270° arc (three-quarter circle), used for round holes defined as 2-vertex polylines
    • bulge = 1.0000 → 180° semicircle
  • Straight segments have bulge = 0
  • No LWPOLYLINE used — template uses legacy POLYLINE with VERTEX sub-entities

docs.title: Round holes as 2-vertex polylines
docs.text:
The template creates circular holes using closed 2-vertex polylines with bulge values, not CIRCLE entities:

vertex 0: (x1, y1) bulge=2.4142  (270° arc)
vertex 1: (x2, y2) bulge=0.4142  (90° arc)

This defines a full circle as two arcs. The 4 corner holes in the template use this pattern.

docs.title: Entity colors
docs.text:
• All entities use color=BYLAYER (inherit from layer)
  • Never set color on individual entities — set it on the layer

docs.title: Geometry notes
docs.text:
• All coordinates in mm
  • Scale 1:1
  • Template crate bounding box: 753 x 615 mm (multiple parts laid out on one sheet)
  • Multiple separate parts (panels) are placed as separate closed polylines
  • Parts can be arranged freely within the 1200 x 600 mm max sheet size

docs.title: Python (ezdxf) example
docs.text:
import ezdxf

doc = ezdxf.new("R2007")
doc.header['$INSUNITS'] = 4      # mm
doc.header['$MEASUREMENT'] = 1   # metric
doc.header['$LUNITS'] = 2        # decimal
doc.header['$LUPREC'] = 3        # 3 decimal places

# Create cut layer — blue, matching Snijlab convention
doc.layers.add("vector-cut", color=5)

msp = doc.modelspace()

# Add a closed polyline (rectangle example)
msp.add_lwpolyline(
    [(0, 0), (100, 0), (100, 50), (0, 50)],
    close=True,
    dxfattribs={"layer": "vector-cut"}
)

doc.saveas("output.dxf")

docs.title: Checklist before uploading to Snijlab
docs.text:
• All cut geometry on vector-cut layer, color 5 (blue)
  • All shapes are closed polylines
  • Units in mm, scale 1:1
  • No text, dimensions, or annotations on the cut layer
  • No overlapping or duplicate cut lines
  • Minimum 2mm spacing between parts (4mm for foam)
  • Total size within 1200 x 600 mm sheet

cf snijlab.nl/en/pages/drawing-rules

docs.title: Bamboo plywood
docs.text: Cross-glued bamboo plywood, FSC 100% certified. Stronger than side-pressed bamboo due to plywood construction. Resists breaking with thin or fragile design elements.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Value Color Warm caramel tone (heat-treated) Cut edges Dark brown Engraved areas Medium brown Grain Cross-glued layers, strong in all directions Moisture Low, prevents warping Max sheet size 1200 x 600 mm

docs.title: Thicknesses used for MacSoccle
docs.text: Thickness Use Price (EUR incl. VAT) 1.5 mm Outside layers €15.08 5 mm Center layers €15.79

docs.title: All available thicknesses
docs.text:
• 0.5 mm — bendable like stiff paper
  • 1.5 mm — thin, rigid
  • 3 mm — rigid
  • 5 mm — highly rigid

cf snijlab.nl/en/products/bamboo-plywood

docs.title: When to nest
docs.text:
• Multiple small parts from the same material
  • Parts that fit conveniently next to or inside each other
  • To reduce waste and production cost
  • Nesting is optional — uploading parts separately with quantity also gives automatic discounts

docs.title: Spacing requirements
docs.text:
Material Minimum gap Standard 2 mm Foam 4 mm

Insufficient spacing causes burn marks and smoke deposits on parts.

docs.title: Shared cutting lines
docs.text:
Parts can share a single cut line along one edge to reduce total cutting length and cost.

Rules for shared lines:

  • Works best for rectangular shapes placed edge-to-edge
  • Remove duplicate lines where shapes connect
  • Do NOT use shared lines for:
    • Small or precision parts (accuracy not guaranteed)
    • Parts with rounded corners
    • Parts requiring dimensional accuracy on the shared edge

docs.title: Creating a nest
docs.text:
1. Set artboard to maximum material dimensions
  2. Arrange parts with proper spacing (see above)
  3. Follow standard drawing rules for layer names and line colors (see specs)
  4. Remove duplicate lines at shared edges

docs.title: Nesting software
docs.text:
• RhinoNest (Rhinoceros plugin) for complex layouts

cf Parts & nests

docs.title: Snijlab specs
docs.text:
• Accepted filetypes: DWG, DXF, AI, PDF
  • Scale: 1:1
  • Units: mm
  • Maximum dimension: 2400 x 1200 mm
  • Outlines: Convert text and fills to outlines
  • Clean files: No size markings, frames, centerlines, etc.
  • Layer name and line color: As described below

docs.title: Layers and colors
docs.text:
Use the appropriate layer name and line color for laser cutting or engraving. The preview in the uploader shows how your drawing is loaded, we produce what the preview shows.

Type Color Layer name Layer Type Cut Blue (rgb 0,0,255) cut lines, polylines, curves, splines Line engraving Red (rgb 255,0,0) line engraving lines, polylines, curves, splines Plane engraving Magenta (rgb 255,0,255) plane engraving closed lines, polylines, curves and splines

docs.title: Drawing tips
docs.text:
• Convert text elements to vector lines
  • Convert shapes to lines according to drawing rules
  • Convert overlapping tessellations to shapes
  • Merge overlapping cut lines
  • Draw a frame around engravings to upload them

cf snijlab.nl/en/pages/drawing-rules

docs.title: MacSoccle
docs.text: A laser-cut bamboo base plate ("socle") for a MacBook Pro 16" M1, with integrated storage for 4 external SSDs and a USB-C hub.

docs.title: Concept
docs.text: A flat horizontal plate that sits under or next to the MacBook Pro, matching its exact footprint and corner rounding. It hides 4 Samsung SSDs and a Satechi USB-C hub inside, connected with short cables, keeping the desk clean.

docs.title: Overall dimensions
docs.text: Parameter Value Width 355.7 mm Depth 248.1 mm Height 16.8 mm Corner radius ~7.25 mm (matching MacBook Pro 16" rounding, ≈ AA battery radius)

docs.title: Layer stack-up
docs.text:
The plate is built from 4 layers of laser-cut bamboo. Layers 1-3 are glued together with wood glue. Layer 4 (top) is removable for access to SSDs.

Layer Thickness Attachment Function 1 (bottom) 1.5 mm glued Solid bottom plate, full footprint 2 5 mm glued Internal frame with cutouts for components 3 5 mm glued Internal frame with cutouts for components 4 (top) 1.5 mm velcro Removable top plate, full footprint

Layer 3 has 16 small holes (10 mm) for velcro dots that connect layer 2 to layer 4 through layer 3. This keeps the velcro inside the stack with no extra height.

  • 4 dots on each side, grouped near corners (~20 mm from corner)
  • Total: 16 velcro dots (10 mm diameter)

Total height: 1.5 + 5 + 5 + 1.5 = 13 mm (~13 mm + rubber feet)

The two outside layers (1 and 4) are 1.5 mm bamboo plywood for a sleek profile. The inside faces of layers 1 and 4 are lined with aluminium foil to spread heat from the SSDs and hub. The two center layers (2 and 3) use a mesh/grid pattern in unused areas to reduce weight.

docs.title: Samsung T7 SSD (x4)
docs.text:
Samsung T7 Portable SSD

Parameter Value Dimensions 85 x 57 x 8 mm Cutout needed 87 x 59 mm per SSD (1 mm clearance) Total height 8 mm (fits within 2 center layers of 5 mm each)

docs.title: Satechi 4-Port USB-C Hub
docs.text:
Satechi 4-Port USB-C Hub with PD (ST-H4CPDM)

Parameter Value Dimensions 60 x 60 x 10 mm Cutout needed 62 x 62 mm (1 mm clearance) Total height 10 mm (fits exactly within 2 center layers of 5 mm each)

docs.title: Cables
docs.text:
• The 4 SSDs connect to the hub internally using the stock Samsung T7 USB-C cables (45 cm, routed path is ~35 cm)
  • Cable channels routed in center layers between hub and SSD positions
  • Only the hub's own USB-C cable exits the soccle, from the middle of one short side (works for left or right USB-C ports on the MacBook)

docs.title: Component layout 1
docs.text:
Hub near cable exit (left short side), 4 SSDs in a 2x2 grid to the right. Hub has 2 USB-C ports on north side (→ SSD1, SSD2) and 2 on south side (→ SSD3, SSD4). Cables run along the top and bottom edges, on the outside of the SSDs.

The two center layers (2 and 3) have: * Component cutouts for SSDs, hub, and hub cable exit * Cable conduit channels (10mm wide) along top/bottom edges and vertical runs to hub * Mesh cutouts in all remaining open areas to minimize weight * Max 10mm solid ribs between cutouts for structural integrity * 30mm solid frame along all edges * Layer 3 additionally has 16 velcro holes (10mm)

docs.title: Component layout reference
docs.text: All coordinates in mm, origin (0,0) = top-left corner of soccle. X axis = width (355.7 mm), Y axis = depth (248.1 mm).

docs.title: Outer plate
docs.text: Parameter Value Width 355.7 Depth 248.1 Corner radius 7.25 Border 5 mm solid frame on all sides

docs.title: USB Hub — Satechi ST-H4CPDM
docs.text: Parameter Value Cutout size 62 x 62 Position (x, y) (20, 93.05) Extent x: 20–82, y: 93.05–155.05 North port 1 (46, 93.05) — connects to SSD1 North port 2 (56, 93.05) — connects to SSD2 South port 1 (46, 155.05) — connects to SSD3 South port 2 (56, 155.05) — connects to SSD4 Cable exit Own USB-C cable exits at x=0, y=119–129 (middle of left short side)

docs.title: SSDs — Samsung T7 x4
docs.text: All SSD cutouts are 87 x 59 mm.

docs.title: SSD 1 (top-left)
docs.text: Parameter Value Position (x, y) (97, 55) Extent x: 97–184, y: 55–114 USB-C connector right side (x=184), centered vertically Hub port north

docs.title: SSD 2 (top-right)
docs.text: Parameter Value Position (x, y) (244, 35) Extent x: 244–331, y: 35–94 USB-C connector left side (x=244), centered vertically Hub port north

docs.title: SSD 3 (bottom-left)
docs.text: Parameter Value Position (x, y) (97, 134) Extent x: 97–184, y: 134–193 USB-C connector right side (x=184), centered vertically Hub port south

docs.title: SSD 4 (bottom-right)
docs.text: Parameter Value Position (x, y) (244, 154) Extent x: 244–331, y: 154–213 USB-C connector left side (x=244), centered vertically Hub port south

docs.title: Gaps
docs.text: Gap Distance Hub to left edge 20 SSD1 ↔ SSD3 (vertical) 20 SSD2 ↔ SSD4 (vertical) 60 SSD columns (horizontal) 60 (x: 184–244)

docs.title: Cable routing
docs.text:
All cables run from SSD connector → center gap → outside edge → back to hub.

Cable Route SSD1 right (x=184) → center gap → up to top edge (y=20) → left to hub north SSD2 left (x=244) → center gap → up to top edge (y=14) → left to hub north SSD3 right (x=184) → center gap → down to bottom edge (y=228) → left to hub south SSD4 left (x=244) → center gap → down to bottom edge (y=234) → left to hub south

docs.title: Velcro dots (16x, 10 mm diameter)
docs.text:
Holes in layer 3, connecting layer 2 to layer 4. 4 per side, grouped near corners (~20 mm from corner).

Side Count Positions (along side) Left (x=8) 4 y: 20, 60, 188.1, 228.1 Right (x=347.7) 4 y: 20, 60, 188.1, 228.1 Top (y=8) 4 x: 20, 60, 295.7, 335.7 Bottom (y=240.1) 4 x: 20, 60, 295.7, 335.7
